text: The surgeon uses near-infrared fluorescence with indocyanine green to visualize and identify the ureter during laparoscopic anterior resection.
During cystoscopy, the surgeon performs left ureteric catheterization and injects indocyanin green retrogradely into the catheter, visibly filling the left ureter with fluorescent dye.

The surgeon uses the Da Vinci XI system to switch between standard white light and near-infrared views, revealing the fluorescent green signal of the indocyanine green-filled ureter, which is easily visible during the laparoscopic anterior resection.
The catheter is positioned externally and connected to a syringe, enabling the surgeon to boost the indocyanin green signal if it fades and facilitating easy removal at the end of the procedure.

The da Vinci XI surgical system utilizes Firefly technology to switch between standard white light and near-infrared illumination, enhancing the visibility and safety of critical structures such as the ureter during laparoscopic anterior resection.
